props.caption: Washington reopens its frontier as China trains one on its own silicon
Europe puts public money behind mass, expendable autonomy

items.note: US and Iranian delegations opened indirect technical talks in Doha through Qatari and Pakistani mediators. Twenty-one South Korean-operated vessels have crossed Hormuz safely since the ceasefire, oil shed its war premium near $72, and the IAEA says inspections will proceed once modalities are set.

Xi Jinping used the Communist Party's anniversary to restate that complete reunification with Taiwan is an unshakable commitment. Reporting read the line as ritual, not a fresh order, even as gray-zone maritime pressure and cross-strait decoupling continue.
Brussels released a €3.9bn tranche for Ukrainian drone procurement as Saab signed a $2.54bn Gripen deal, financing the mass-autonomy industrial base Kyiv's war has forced into being.
The Rapid Support Forces kept up pressure around El Obeid in North Kordofan amid warnings of mass-atrocity risk. The prevention window stayed open only because the city had not fallen.

items.what: South Korea's June exports posted their strongest growth since 1978, up 70.9% year on year, led by a 199.5% surge in chip shipments — the AI-hardware boom showing up in a whole country's trade data.

China's official manufacturing PMI rose to 50.3 in June, a third month of expansion led by high-tech exports, even as the mandatory USMCA review opened in Washington and started a decade-long sunset clock.
Europe's record heatwave turned political: France's government faced a no-confidence motion over its handling, and Spain attributed more than 1,000 excess deaths to its second-hottest June on record.

Bloom Energy and Brookfield expanded an AI-data-centre power partnership to $25 billion, and Britain's National Grid took a $1.75 billion stake in a US venture — electricity, not just chips, is the binding constraint on compute.
The US military deployed more than 900 personnel to Venezuela's earthquake response as the toll passed an estimated 1,900 a week on, with the count still officially unverified.
Pakistan said it shot down four drones launched from Afghanistan into Balochistan as the Taliban claimed retaliatory airstrikes, extending a cross-border cycle that no mediator has yet contained.
